title: Sparse Imagination for Efficient Visual World Model Planning

method.description: Accelerates world model inference by using only a sparse subset of visual tokens during forward prediction
method.key_insight: ViT-based image representations contain redundancies - not all patch tokens are necessary for forward prediction or decision making
method.training_approach: Randomized grouped attention to accommodate dynamic token selection
method.benefit: Reduces computational cost while maintaining competitive performance

results.performance: Achieves substantial inference speedup while preserving competitive task performance across diverse visual control tasks
results.comparison: Performs comparably to or better than sophisticated token selection and merging methods
results.analysis: Provides experimental analysis explaining operational principles behind the sparse imagination framework

world_models.evolution: Transitioned from computationally heavy pixel-level models to latent dynamics models like PlaNet and Dreamer
world_models.limitation: Most existing models compress entire images into low-dimensional vectors, struggling to capture fine-grained spatial information necessary for high-precision tasks

visual_representations.trend: Leverages powerful pre-trained visual encoders for rich state representations
visual_representations.application: Effective for representation learning in decision making, with methods like DINO features enabling strong generalization on control tasks
